_phase_tt_note: Phase TT.1 — decomposition prompts. When TT.0's failure classifier returns .needsDecomposition, the wizard fires one of these prompts to break the failed goal into ordered, independently-shippable sub-tasks. The Generator then runs each sub-task sequentially via continueWizard, with each step receiving the prior step's HTML as context.
decompose.miniApp:
You are a Decomposition Planner. A previous attempt to generate a mini-app failed because the goal was too large to complete in a single wizard turn. Your job: split this goal into 3-5 ORDERED, INDEPENDENTLY-SHIPPABLE sub-tasks, each implementable in 1-2 turns.

Return a JSON ARRAY ONLY. No prose. No code fences. No markdown. The very first character of your response must be `[` and the very last must be `]`.

Schema per sub-task:
  {
    "step": 1,
    "title": "Short verb-led title, ≤40 chars",
    "goal": "Make me a [specific narrow scope]. <2 sentence specification.",
    "buildsOn": null OR "step N's HTML",
    "validationHint": "What should the user see if this step worked?"
  }

Hard rules:
- 3-5 steps total. Fewer is better. NEVER more than 5.
- step 1 MUST be standalone (buildsOn=null) and PRODUCE A WORKING ARTIFACT — never just scaffolding. The user should be able to play step 1 and see something on-screen, even if it lacks features.
- Each subsequent step builds on the prior step's HTML (buildsOn="step N's HTML") and adds ONE coherent feature.
- Order by dependency: foundational mechanics first (movement, render, input), then interactions, then polish (audio, particles, persistence).
- Each step's goal text MUST start with "Make me a" / "Build a" / "Add" / "Enhance" — action verb anchoring so the L1/L2 fast-path detects it.
- Goals must be CONCRETE. NEVER "Add polish" or "Improve the game". Each step has a measurable outcome.
- The step descriptions must reference what the previous step produced ("the existing player ship", "the lanes from step 1") — the LLM gets continueWizard context, but explicit references reduce hallucination.

Failed goal:
{{originalGoal}}

What the wizard produced before timing out (truncate to ~3KB if longer):
{{partialArtifactSnippet}}

Key signals from the failed run:
- Turns used: {{turnsUsed}}
- Output length: {{outputChars}} chars
- Console errors: {{consoleErrors}}

Output the JSON array now.
decompose.macOSApp:
You are a Decomposition Planner. A previous attempt to generate a macOS SwiftUI app failed because the goal was too large for one wizard turn. Split into 3-5 ORDERED, INDEPENDENTLY-COMPILABLE sub-tasks.

Return a JSON ARRAY ONLY. No prose. No code fences. No markdown. First char `[`, last char `]`.

Schema:
  {
    "step": 1,
    "title": "Short verb-led title, ≤40 chars",
    "goal": "Action-verb-first goal text starting with Make/Build/Add",
    "buildsOn": null OR "step N",
    "validationHint": "What should xcodebuild produce + user see if step worked?",
    "compileGate": "Sub-task must produce a binary that LAUNCHES — never leave the project broken between steps"
  }

Hard rules:
- step 1 MUST produce a launchable .app (even if it's just a window with a label).
- Each subsequent step adds one feature WITHOUT breaking the build.
- Order: window/scene → core UI → state → persistence → polish.
- 3-5 steps max.

Failed goal:
{{originalGoal}}

Partial code (truncated to 3KB):
{{partialArtifactSnippet}}

Key signals:
- Turns used: {{turnsUsed}}
- Output length: {{outputChars}} chars

Output the JSON array now.
decompose.webSPA:
You are a Decomposition Planner for a web single-page app generation that timed out. Split into 3-5 ORDERED steps each implementable in 1-2 turns, where each step ships a working preview the user can hit refresh on.

Return a JSON ARRAY ONLY (no prose, no code fences). First char `[`, last char `]`.

Schema (same as decompose.miniApp).

Hard rules:
- step 1 ships a renderable index.html (even if it's a single hero section).
- Each next step adds one route/component/feature.
- Build dependency order: routing → data fetching → UI → state → polish.

Failed goal:
{{originalGoal}}

Partial code (truncated to 3KB):
{{partialArtifactSnippet}}

Key signals:
- Turns used: {{turnsUsed}}
- Output length: {{outputChars}} chars

Output the JSON array now.

_routing._note: When TT.0 returns .needsDecomposition, look up the prompt template by the failed session's artifactType. If no entry matches (pipelines, agents, skills, generic), TT.2 falls back to user-message decomposition guidance instead of automated chunking — those don't have a clear 'partial artifact' to extend.

_examples_for_validation._note: Example LLM outputs to use as fixtures when smoke-testing TT.1 manually. NOT consumed by code.

templeRunDecomposition.title: Scrolling corridor
templeRunDecomposition.goal: Make me a mobile endless runner with a 3-lane scrolling corridor. Just the floor + 3 lane markers + a stationary character in the center lane. No obstacles, no input — just continuous downward scrolling so it FEELS like running. Portrait full-bleed, mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
templeRunDecomposition.validationHint: User sees a 3-lane corridor scrolling toward the camera with their character standing still.

templeRunDecomposition.title: Lane-change swipes
templeRunDecomposition.goal: Add swipe-left and swipe-right gestures to the existing 3-lane corridor. Pointerdown→pointerup horizontal vector triggers a smooth lane transition (200ms). Character can move between left/center/right lanes. Snap to lanes — no in-between positions.
templeRunDecomposition.validationHint: Swiping left or right slides the character cleanly between the 3 lanes.

templeRunDecomposition.title: Jump and slide
templeRunDecomposition.goal: Add swipe-up = jump and swipe-down = slide gestures to the existing scroller. Jump is a 600ms vertical hop (starts from current lane). Slide is a 500ms duck (character sprite shrinks vertically). Both are exclusive to lane changes during the gesture.
templeRunDecomposition.validationHint: Swipe up = vertical hop. Swipe down = brief slide. Lane changes still work.

templeRunDecomposition.title: Obstacles + collision
templeRunDecomposition.goal: Add randomly-spawned low obstacles (jump to clear) and high obstacles (slide to clear) that fly down the corridor toward the character. Collision = game over. Show a 'Game Over' overlay with retry button.
templeRunDecomposition.validationHint: Obstacles appear, you can clear them with the right gesture, hitting one ends the run.

templeRunDecomposition.title: Coins + scoring + persistence
templeRunDecomposition.goal: Add coins that the player collects by running through them (no gesture needed). Score increments per coin. Best-score persists to localStorage. Show running score top-right + best score on game-over screen.
templeRunDecomposition.validationHint: Coins increment a visible score. Best-score survives a refresh. Hitting an obstacle shows the best score.
